item.title: No matter what kind of girl you are, JoCat will love and support you
item.description:
If you don't know JoCat, if nothing else you probably remember him being depicted in the "I Like Girls" video:
I hope he's doing well. If you don't know, at the end of last year there was a bit of an online and somewhat offline harassment campaign against him, and he stepped back from internet activity and making content.

item.title: Band of Brothers biggest error.
item.description:
Albert Blithe was the main character for the third episode of the HBO series "Band of Brothers". It was believed that he passed from his wounds since E Company did not know what happened to him. After the shows release, the family of Blithe announced to the publisher that this wasn't true.
After receiving the Purple Heart Blithe actually recovered from his wound but was discharged from the war. After his discharge he found himself a job at Westinghouse Electrics, he then later re-enlisted in 1949 and 1954 where he served with the 187th airborne. He later married a woman named Kay and had 2 kids, a son and a daughter. On 1967 he died of a perforated ulcer after a trip to commemorate the Battle of Bulge. I thought I should Post this so he could get the recognition he deserves since his life was only shown in the republished version of the novel. TLDR: Blithe didn't die in war and got married and had kids. Still a great show though!
item.dc:creator: barkmank1

item.title: Cel Shading misconception
item.description:
New trophy available, I wanted to participate while sharing something related to my field as a game artist. I thought "hey why not cel shading/borderlands" The common misconception is that Borderlands games are Cel Shading because of their outline aesthetics but this is not what it is.
Cell shading is basically just the shadows being clamped to different values in rendering. The balls above are showing a simple example (Picture taken from google search)
This screenshot is from Unreal Marketplace (taken from google search)
Zelda games are very often using cel shading. It's a rendering style that is more often used alongside very minimal textures. It works well with solid colors as it creates a sort of a comic book coloring style.
Windwaker is also Cel Shading.
Jet Set Radio Future (2002, classic Xbox) was also using Cel shading and they mixed it with the infamous black outlines as they often work very well together, enhancing the comic book style effect. The occasional rendering mix of black outlines with cel shading are what, in my opinion, created the misconception that outlines = cel shading.
Dragonball games are also very big on pairing Cel Shading with geo/textures outlines.
My research on Borderlands games art development is not extensive but in my opinion, its simply models outlines rendered paired with actual lines drawn on the texture files. The shading itself has no Cel Shading style clamping. It doesn't take away anything from the Borderlands games aesthetic and style. It's great visuals, just not what its commonly called
Thank you for coming to my ted talk
item.dc:creator: ostranaut

item.title: Trying to be okay with my body.
item.description:
Hey everyone! So I have been on a journey with my weight for years now trying to feel good in my own body and I feel like this is the perfect place to vent my frustrations. Sorry in advance for the many selfies and I appreciate all of you for listening to me. This is the skinniest I let myself get at under 120 lbs about a year ago. From my previous posts I discussed that I have struggled with my weight after having kids and have been trying to get it right. I want to love myself to teach my kids to do the same in life. It is incredibly hard when I have been my own worst enemy for years when it comes to insecurities. I know we all have them and if you are reading this and feel the same way just know you are not alone and I see you.
When I weighed 120 people around me seemed concerned and tried to feed me all of the time which drove me absolutely crazy. When I was my heaviest I felt guilty eating a cookie and then at this weight I had love ones, strangers and friends throwing food at me like I didn't know how to eat. I know that seems like a weird thing to complain about but it made me feel like there was something wrong with me for being smaller than I used to be instead of good about the body I was in. I just wanted my weight to not be a part of any discussion whether it was about being too big or too small.
This is from early fall of 2023. I started to gain weight again gradually thinking the conversations about my weight would stop because I was back to almost 130 lbs and healthy. I was wrong. I don't know what makes certain people think it is okay to discuss ones body but it drives me bananas.
This is from winter time this year at around 140 lbs and honestly I think this is the weight that I feel the most comfortable in. Not too big and not too small. I did have days this winter where I felt like a fat sausage and got really frustrated with myself.
This is me today.
I am anywhere between 130 and 140 lbs these passed couple of months and some days I still feel gross but for the most part I try my absolute best to love myself. I want my son and daughter to look at themselves in the mirror with kind eyes so I am trying my best to do the same. If you are stuck struggling with your weight or see a post of some magically skinny person, just know that their journey doesn't end at their goal weight. It is a mental and physical struggle every day to maintain a healthy weight and a healthy relationship with one's self regarding weight. I have been all over the place with mine over the past 7 years but at the moment I think that this is the most manageable me I can be. Thank you for letting me vent this out here and even if this dies in usersub I needed to voice this somewhere so thank you imgur for existing. Please love yourself and I will do my best to also do the same. We can do this!
item.dc:creator: aticklepickle

item.title: Price-Fixing Case Shows Need for Aggressive Big Oil Investigations
item.description: Since day one of his administration, the fossil fuel industry and its right-wing allies have disingenuously blamed President Biden for increases in fuel prices, falsely claiming the administration was holding back fossil fuel production. Now, evidence is mounting that Big Oil may have conspired behind the scenes to sock American consumers with higher prices at the pump. In a blockbuster civil settlement reached in May, the Federal Trade Commission revealed that the former CEO of Texas’ largest oil drilling company sent hundreds of text messages to OPEC representatives, allegedly intending to hold down oil production. Neither Scott Sheffield, the oil tycoon, nor his former company, Pioneer Natural Resources, are household names outside the Texas oil industry. Nevertheless, Pioneer and other large independent oil and gas producers compete with international oil majors such as Chevron and Exxon, ensuring that worldwide oil prices reflect genuine supply-and-demand dynamics rather than illegal market manipulation. If Pioneer sought to collude with OPEC nations to limit oil production, as the FTC alleges, that would push oil prices and oil company profits through the roof, as has been true for the last three years. The FTC has referred the case to the Department of Justice for a potential criminal prosecution. Accusing Sheffield of a “sustained and long-running strategy to coordinate output reductions,” the FTC blocked Sheffield from serving on the board of ExxonMobil, which acquired Pioneer for nearly $60 billion. Pioneer denies that Sheffield’s statements were intended to circumvent competition laws. Keeping a lid on production allows Big Oil to reap monster profits at the expense of the driving public. The ultra-wealthy leaders of the OPEC oil cartel, the 12-country organization that controls nearly 80 percent of global crude oil reserves, have the power to move prices up and down based on production levels. Ten more countries, including Russia, are known as OPEC+ and collaborate with the cartel. Sheffield seems to have preferred an even larger cartel, drawing Texas’ oil producers into collusion with the OPEC+ nations. “If Texas leads the way, maybe we can get OPEC to cut production,” the FTC reports Sheffield as saying. Maybe Saudi and Russia will follow. That was our plan,” he said, adding: “I was using the tactics of OPEC+ to get a bigger OPEC+ done.” Sheffield is not alone in advocating for production limits. Many oil and gas giants spent several years publicly arguing about the need to dial back production at a time of low prices. For example, in 2016, oil billionaire Harold Hamm, a close ally of former president Donald Trump, called for Russia and OPEC to dial back output and end their price war, which was holding prices down. “I think it would be high time for them to come to an agreement,” Hamm said. “U.S. producers have cut back, we’ve done our part, it would finally make sense for a freeze in production to be implemented.” The public needs to learn more about potential industry collusion to limit production. Democrats on the House Energy and Commerce Committee have launched an investigation into the behavior of seven oil and gas companies, including BP, Chevron, Shell and Occidental Petroleum, and numerous Democratic senators are asking Justice and the FTC to probe further. Congress must investigate the content of publicly disclosed meetings between OPEC and U.S. oil producers at which production limits may have been discussed in private. Indeed, after meeting with U.S. shale players at a dinner in Houston, Gabriel Mbaga Obiang Lima, a top official from Equatorial Guinea, a prominent African oil producer, told Reuters: “The key thing is that information is shared about our projections; it really helps everybody.” The FTC, Congress and Justice must aggressively investigate the extent of oil industry collusion and whether it violated civil and criminal laws. Though there’s still a lot we don’t yet know, there’s a lot we do: —While Big Oil has decried the Biden administration for limiting U.S. oil production, in reality, U.S. fossil fuel production has soared under Biden to the highest output in history, largely thanks to U.S. oil exports that hurt Americans. —In 2021 and 2022, surging demand combined with restricted supply sent the price of oil soaring and led to unfathomable profits. The top five oil companies made almost $200 billion in profits in 2022 at the direct expense of consumers in the United States and worldwide. —The formula for this massive rip-off of the public was simple: prices soared, production costs remained flat, consumers suffered and Big Oil lined its pockets. Meanwhile, Big Oil deployed its political power to defeat a simple measure — windfall profit taxes — that could have returned the excessive profits to consumers. We know that Pioneer CEO Sheffield pursued a strategy to reduce output and raise prices and wasn’t shy about saying so publicly. The truth is this: It’s not the Biden administration that drove up oil prices. It was the greed of Big Oil. Now it’s time to find out if that greed was coupled with illegal collusion. https://dcjournal.com/price-fixing-case-shows-need-for-aggressive-big-oil-investigations/ #PriceFixing
item.dc:creator: doxydad1
